Home

Threat Actors Exploiting Recently Disclosed Vulnerability

CISA, FBI, and partners are releasing this joint Cybersecurity Advisory to warn organizations about ongoing exploitation activity.

Threat actors have been observed gaining initial access via T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), followed by execution of T1059.001 (PowerShell). After establishing persistence using T1543.003 (Windows Service), the actors use T1003 to dump credentials.

Lateral movement has been observed via T1021.001 (RDP) and T1021.006 (WinRM). The actors collect data using T1005 and exfiltrate via T1041.

For tactical context, the activity maps to the Initial Access tactic [TA0001] and Credential Access [TA0006]. Reference the MITRE ATT&CK framework for additional detail.

Mitigations

Apply patches promptly. Restrict outbound traffic. Monitor for the techniques described above.
